title: TUI transcript blocks: toggle-collapsible reasoning/tool blocks + copyable markdown/prompt renderer + code & diff rendering

AC1.note: attachBlockIo (tui-shell.ts) makes the real IO wiring reachable; test 'AC1: the REAL io wiring retains a tool result full output' drives runAgentTurn through createTuiAgentIo + attachBlockIo and asserts registry.bodyText(id) equals the tool output. The mountBlockHarness replica was retired in 7ca9421.

AC2.note: createBlockRegistry.toggle is per block: transcript-blocks.test.ts registers 3 blocks, toggles the middle one, asserts the others unchanged. Markers pinned by blockLabel tests in md-blocks.test.ts and asserted in a captured frame in tui-shell.test.ts.

AC3.note: tui-shell.test.ts drive-through via real createTestRenderer + mockInput through the real onKeypress: Ctrl+O enters, arrows move, Enter expands (body visible, marker flips), Esc exits and the composer regains focus. Frame-level assertions, not state-only.

AC4.note: tui-shell.test.ts asserts the frame is byte-identical while isBlocked() is true (menu/overlay), and that a turn completing mid-nav does not steal focus (focusOwner guard, focusComposer no-ops).

AC5.note: Assistant markdown is segmented via segmentMarkdown; a fenced segment renders framed with a language tag and line count. Test asserts 'ts - 2 lines' in the captured frame and no backticks, driven through runAgentTurn.

AC6.note: y copies the focused block bodyText via copyToClipboardOSC52 with the Copied to clipboard toast; copy refuses and toasts truthfully for evicted/unknown blocks (F5 fix). /expand and /copy registered in src/commands/agent-commands.ts.

AC7.note: Diff line classes asserted on captureSpans() foreground RGBA toInts(), not substrings, for a diff fence; the '- ' bullet-list negative is proven both in md-blocks.test.ts (looksLikeUnifiedDiff false) and in the TUI and readline render tests.

AC8.note: Retention bounded by maxBlocks and maxRetainedChars; the review found the newest-block exemption made the char cap escapable, fixed in 7ca9421 by clip-on-register (prefix retained, marked truncated). Four tests incl. a 5000-char payload against a 100-char cap asserting retainedChars() <= cap. Evicted blocks keep their summary and expand to EVICTED_BLOCK_TEXT. Plan D-4 amended to the delivered semantics.

AC9.note: package.json and bun.lock diffs are empty. keryx ctx rg CodeRenderable|DiffRenderable|MarkdownRenderable|TreeSitterClient over src/** returns 4 matches, all comments. keryx ctx rg @opentui over src/lib/** returns 0. src/lib/md-blocks.ts has zero imports. No tree-sitter worker and no render-time grammar fetch introduced.

AC10.note: Readline /expand keeps its behavior but is now built on the exported expandedToolOutput, which takes its header from blockLabel and routes a looksLikeUnifiedDiff body through renderDiff - the same src/lib helpers the TUI uses, so the two cannot drift. Eight tests in shell.test.ts incl. the shared-blockLabel case, the AC7 bullet negative and a NO_COLOR case.

AC11.note: Every new transcript container uses flexShrink 0 with alignSelf flex-start and no unconstrained flexGrow. Tested structurally at four terminal sizes after expanding a 120-line block: footer is the last row, the composer box occupies the three rows above it, the draft survives. One carve-out at scrollTop === 2 for an upstream OpenTUI ScrollBox border-clipping defect, reproduced from pure OpenTUI primitives with zero keryx code and pinned by a test that fails when upstream fixes it. Recorded in specification 7.1.

AC12.note: Expanding a non-newest block asserts scrollHeight grew, scrollTop is unchanged and stickyScroll is false, with a newest-block control case. Determinism via the injectable schedule introduced in b2c25e6.

AC13.note: bun test full suite: 2012 pass / 11 skip / 0 fail across 231 files. bun run typecheck clean. review-orchestrator produced 1 HIGH, 3 MEDIUM, 5 LOW; all fixed in d523e8e and 7ca9421, with every disposition and two deliberate deferrals (paint performance, collapseToolOutput CRLF) recorded in journal.md. Code Health gate WARN at score 92 is pre-existing against a stale 2026-07-06 baseline: zero P0/P1, regression 3 vs the 5 already recorded in flow 001, and a changed-scope run attributes nothing to this branch.

AC14.note: specification.md section 3 replaces the stale inline-expand bullet with the delivered behavior and the full key table; new section 7.1 records the harness facts (sequential @opentui import, the 20ms Esc timeout, the ScrollBox bleed); new section 9 records D-2 with claims verified against the vendored package (new Worker -> parser.worker.js, grammar loader fetches a URL, five bundled grammars) plus the revisit condition, alongside D-3 and D-5.
